commit.message:
fix: SOL always missing from /portfolio — native balance has no mint field

SOL's wallet entry { symbol:'SOL', quantity:X } has no mint field, so it
bypasses the two mint-gated fallback layers (lines 61 + 76) and lands at
price=0 → value=0 → filtered by h.value >= 0.01.

Fix in routes/portfolio.js:
- After initial holdings map, explicitly check if SOL has price=0 and fetch
  it directly via fetchPricesByMint([SOL_MINT]) — no mint field required
- Add || h.token === 'SOL' to the final filter so native SOL balance is
  never silently dropped even if Jupiter pricing temporarily fails

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(books): convert added_at epoch ms to ISO string for TIMESTAMPTZ column

Same bug as movies: bookToRow was passing Date.now() (e.g. 1741644000000)
directly to added_at, but books.added_at is TIMESTAMPTZ in Supabase.
Postgres rejects the raw integer with "date/time field value out of range".

Fix mirrors the movies fix: new Date(b.addedAt).toISOString() converts
any legacy number timestamp or ISO string correctly before sending.

commit.message:
Optimize dashboardCategory endpoint - fix N+1 query performance issue

Performance Improvement:
- Replace N+1 query pattern with single LEFT JOIN query
- Before: 1 + N queries (1 for categories + 1 for each category subscription check)
- After: 1 single query with LEFT JOIN
- Significantly faster category loading (50 categories: 51 queries → 1 query)

Changes:
- Use DB query builder with LEFT JOIN on transaction_details
- Calculate isSubscribed in SQL using CASE statement
- Maintain same response format for backward compatibility

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove Anthropic key from setup flow; only LogiQ key needed

- keys setup: prompt for LogiQ key only (used for both Claude + Codex)
- keys status/delete: remove all anthropic key references
- aiswitch status: show single LogiQ key entry
- Remove unused _aiswitch_anthropic_key() function

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update beam optics tutorial to F2 IR spec: 30 µm spot, 0.66 mm Rayleigh range

- w₀: 0.05 mm → 0.015 mm (30 µm diameter spot per F2 5W IR spec)
- z_R: 7.4 mm → 0.664 mm (recalculated)
- Depth of field: ~10–30 mm → ~1.33 mm
- zRange: ±22 mm → ±12 mm (more appropriate scale)
- ys scale: 520 → 260 (rebalanced for new beam dimensions)
- Tables and summary updated with accurate F2 IR values
- Added 15W diode vs IR spot comparison in divergence text

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add diffview.nvim for git diff and file history

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: SOL missing from portfolio — fetch price from Jupiter, not DexScreener

DexScreener's /tokens/{mint} batch endpoint returns no pairs for the native
SOL mint because SOL is almost always the quote token in Solana pairs.
This caused market['SOL'] to be undefined → price=0 → value=0 → filtered
out by the h.value >= 0.01 gate in portfolio.js.

- Add fetchSolPriceJupiter() with 60s cache, fetches in parallel with the
  DexScreener ecosystem batch
- fetchSolanaMarketMap: guarantee map['SOL'] is always populated; if
  DexScreener happened to return SOL pair data, keep its change1h/24h
  but override price with Jupiter (more accurate); otherwise create a
  SOL-only entry from Jupiter price
- Remove SOL from TOP_SOLANA_MINTS — dedicated Jupiter fetch is more reliable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: enforce tenant isolation to prevent cross-tenant data leaks

- TeamSelector: scope localStorage keys by tenant hostname to prevent
  boards from one company appearing for another
- database.js: make _checkTenantColumn per-table (was only checking
  metrics_history, not boards_cache)
- database.js: use _applyTenantFilter in getCachedBoards instead of
  inline logic that returned any tenant's data when tenantId was null
- database.js: fix saveBoards to not delete ALL tenants' cache when
  tenantId is null (only delete NULL tenant_id rows)
- App.jsx: clear ALL scrum-dashboard-* localStorage keys on logout
  to prevent stale cross-tenant data on next login

commit.message:
Add AI-generated match analysis via Claude API

- New app/services/analysis.py: generates individual match analysis text
  using claude-haiku for each match in parallel (asyncio.gather)
- Uses quantitative features (Elo, form, H2H, position, rest, DC ratings)
  to explain WHY the model predicts what it does
- In-memory cache per (matchday, matchId) to avoid regenerating on refresh
- Graceful fallback: returns empty string if API key not set
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY added to config (set via Railway env var)
- analysisText: str | None added to MatchPrediction schema
- iOS: analysisText field added to MatchPrediction model
- iOS: "KI-Analyse" card shown in MatchDetailView when text is available

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: re-dispatch planning zouzhe after timeout retry

check_timeouts clears dispatched_at on retry, but poll_and_dispatch
only picks up created→planning and revising→planning transitions.
A zouzhe already in 'planning' state with dispatched_at=NULL (from
retry) was never re-dispatched. Add step 2a to handle this case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(movies): convert added_at to ISO string for TIMESTAMPTZ column

movies.added_at is TIMESTAMPTZ in Supabase but JS was sending raw epoch
milliseconds (Date.now() = e.g. 1741644000000) which Postgres rejects as
out-of-range for a timestamp. Also fixed the movies table in Supabase:
the pre-existing schema had id as UUID but JS generates text IDs like
'mv_1741644000000' — dropped and recreated with id TEXT.

movieToRow() now converts: m.addedAt ? new Date(m.addedAt).toISOString()
so both legacy number timestamps and ISO strings round-trip correctly.

Both movies and books INSERT/SELECT verified via REST API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: log missing DexScreener pairs, auto-include held positions, USDC hardcoded

- fetchSolanaEcosystem: log [No pair found for: SYMBOL (mint...)] for any
  hardcoded token that returns no DexScreener pairs, making gaps visible
- loadHeldSupplementalMints: reads entry-prices.json + token-registry-cache.json
  on each fetch to auto-include held positions not in TOP_SOLANA_MINTS —
  EAT (375ai mint) and ZAUTH (zauthx402 mint) will now be batch-fetched
- fetchSolanaMarketMap: inject USDC_ENTRY (price=1) directly — USDC never
  falls through to Jupiter fallback pricing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(db): add movies, books, reading_sessions tables (migration 004)

Root cause of books/movies not persisting: these Supabase tables
were never created. Both pages silently failed on upsert and returned
empty arrays on load.

Tables added:
- movies: id, user_id, tmdb_id, title, poster_path, release_year,
          hype, watched, added_at (BIGINT epoch ms)
- books:  id, user_id, ol_key, title, author, cover_id, total_pages,
          pages_read, status, notes, added_at, updated_at
          + set_updated_at() trigger (keeps homepage widget sorted)
- reading_sessions: id (identity), user_id, book_id (FK→books),
                    session_date, pages_read, notes

Migration applied to Supabase manually via SQL Editor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add smart coach dropdown to schedule dialog

When creating/editing a schedule, the coach dropdown now auto-fetches
only coaches that are available for the selected class, day, and time
(based on compensation rules, availability, and conflict detection).
Shows "Sin coach" option to allow creating schedules without a coach.

commit.message:
Guard all Purchases.shared access with isConfigured check

Previous fix only guarded the delegate assignment in init(), but
loadProducts, checkSubscriptionStatus, restorePurchases, purchaseSubscription,
and setTikTokAttribution all accessed Purchases.shared without checking
isConfigured first — causing fatal crash on iPad when SubscriptionManager
singleton initializes before Purchases.configure() completes.
